content:
The Thunderbird mobile app doesn't have it's own address book or sync mechanism for Android contacts. You will need to look into third party android apps.

If your email provider supports carddav, an app such as Davx5 might be a possible solution.

F-Droid: https://f-droid.org/packages/at.bitfire.davdroid/ Play store: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=at.bitfire.davdroid&hl=en-US

The idea for a built-in address book has been requested by the community on mozilla connect. You can show support by voting for this, or raise your own ideas and suggestions.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/an-address-book-in-thunderbird-for-android/idi-p/84952
creator: platform34

content:
The Unified account is normally automatically enabled when multiple accounts are present. You can toggle the feature on/off from: Settings > General Settings > Display > Show Unified Inbox.

Once enabled, the Unified account is displayed at the top of the account list.

  1. Press the top left menu button or swipe right on the message list.
  2. Tap the down/up arrow (top left of screen) to toggle between the account list and account folder view.

If you have custom imap folder and wish it's content to display in the Unified Inbox ...

  1. Press the top left menu button.
  2. Select an account with it's folder list visible (inbox, draft etc).
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list).
  4. Tap a folders name.
  5. Toggle "Unify" ON.
creator: platform34
